assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism limit (~20 nodes simultaneously)' and explains this constraint is independent of other settings

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: Response provides exact calculation: '3,000 nodes ÷ 20 concurrent = ~150 batches minimum' and shows this requires 12.5-25 hours vs 8-hour window

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Response recommends extending to 36-hour monthly window and provides phased upgrade execution plan across multiple time periods

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Phase 1 - CPU pools first (lower risk)' and provides specific command examples for sequential pool upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: Response states 'Schedule GPU pool upgrades when no active training jobs' and suggests upgrading training pools 'during scheduled gaps between runs'

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Response recommends extending maintenance window to 36 hours and provides gcloud command to configure monthly Saturday 2am-2pm window

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Response does not mention cluster autoscaler scaling limitations or how autoscaler interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the 15,000 node standard limit or the increased 65,000 node limit for AI workloads in GKE 1.31+
